title: Worcester man receives life sentence for home invasion
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Concepcion Rodriguez, 39, was sentenced to life in prison for a 2022 home invasion in Fitchburg.
Rodriguez faced additional charges including armed assault, firearm possession, and larceny, according to the DA's office.
Rodriguez's attorney intends to appeal, arguing the habitual criminal penalty violated eighth amendment rights.

title: Derrick White talks beer, being a Bostonian, and a promising Celtics playoff run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White joined Julian Edelman, Zdeno Chara, and Garrett Crochet for the launch of Samuel Adams' 'Our City. Our Beer.' line.
White, feeling like a Bostonian, celebrates the Celtics' potential playoff success and reflects on his connection with fans.
The Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference with a 53-25 record, poised for another title run with Tatum's return.

title: Mike Krzyzewski threw a subtle jab at Bill Belichick in response to new UNC hire
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: UNC hired former Nuggets coach Michael Malone to replace Hubert Davis as the men's basketball coach.
Mike Krzyzewski commented on UNC's hiring of Malone, highlighting the challenges of transitioning from pro to college coaching.
Bill Belichick is UNC's head football coach, aiming to model the program after the NFL experience.

title: Person stabs dog in Milton during alleged attack
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A person in Milton stabbed a dog allegedly attacking them on Blue Hill River Road, State Police reported.
The dog, which was off-leash at the time of the incident, was taken to a local veterinarian.
Milton Animal Control is investigating the stabbing incident involving the dog and the person.

title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora moved Trevor Story to No. 5 in the lineup due to recent struggles.
Masataka Yoshida replaces Story at the No. 2 spot, with Roman Anthony remaining the leadoff hitter.
Boston averages 3.3 runs per game, with a low 8.4% walk rate and high 27.8% strikeout rate.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers prefer the No. 4 seed to avoid a second-round matchup with the Boston Celtics.
ESPN Cleveland's Andrew Goldhammer claims the Cavs are 'scared' of facing the Celtics in the playoffs.
The Celtics won all three regular-season games against Cleveland and currently hold the No. 2 seed.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts residents pay varying electricity rates, with municipal utility customers often paying half of those served by investor-owned companies.
Eversource in Massachusetts reported 16 cents profit per dollar paid by customers, totaling $630.6 million in 2025.
Municipal utilities keep costs down with factors like smaller service areas and long-term contracts, but lack Mass Save eligibility.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students protest lack of timely communication from the university following violent incidents near campus.
A recent stabbing and fatal police shooting near campus heightened concerns among students about safety updates.
Students urge Northeastern to send broader alerts and increase transparency about both on-campus and off-campus incidents.

title: BU president pauses signage policy after Pride flag controversy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: BU President Melissa Gilliam pauses signage policy affecting Pride flags after months of protests and backlash.
The controversial policy prohibited materials like Pride flags on outward-facing university property, sparking protests and petitions.
Gilliam apologizes for the impact on LGBTQ+ students and calls for community dialogue on policy and core values.

title: Red Sox complete Sonny Gray trade, send pitching prospect to Cardinals
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Red Sox completed the Sonny Gray trade by sending pitching prospect Patrick Galle to the St. Louis Cardinals.
Patrick Galle, a 22-year-old right-handed pitcher, was picked by the Red Sox from Ole Miss in the 17th round of last year's draft.
Sonny Gray is a three-time All-Star with a 4.50 ERA this season and will start against the Brewers on Tuesday.
